title: Generic Maximum with Printable Types
prompt_md:
## Goal

Write a function template `maxOf` that returns the larger of two values using `operator>`, then make a custom `Score` class work with it. This is the lesson's core point: a template that uses an operator works for any type that overloads that operator, and `operator<<` is found via ADL when defined as a friend inside the class.

## Input

- One line with two integers, the values of two `Score` objects.

## Output

- One line: the larger `Score`, printed via `operator<<`.

Requirements: `maxOf` is `template <typename T> T maxOf(T a, T b)` returning `a` when `a > b` else `b`. `Score` overloads `operator>` (as a member) comparing its stored value, and `operator<<` (as a friend defined inside the class body) printing that value. When the two scores are equal, `maxOf` returns the second.

title: Generic Accumulate with a Wrapper Class Template
prompt_md:
## Goal

Write a class template `Val<T>` that wraps a single value and supports `+` and stream output, then write a function template `accumulate3` that sums three of them. The lesson point: operator overloads (including a friend `operator<<` defined inline in a class template) work seamlessly inside other templates.

## Input

- One line with three integers.

## Output

- One line: the sum of the three values, printed via `operator<<` on a `Val<int>`.

Requirements: `template <typename T> class Val` stores a `T`, overloads `operator+` (member) returning a new `Val<T>`, and overloads `operator<<` as a friend defined inline in the class body. `accumulate3` is `template <typename T> T accumulate3(T a, T b, T c)` returning `a + b + c`.
